title: Utah Drivers Ed Practice
source: 2025–2026 Utah Driver Handbook (Utah Driver License Division)

questions.question: What is the minimum age to apply for an entry-level learner permit in Utah?
questions.explanation: You must have reached a minimum age of 15 to be entitled to drive with an entry-level learner permit.

questions.question: An entry-level learner permit's written knowledge test scores are valid for how many months?
questions.explanation: Written knowledge test scores for an entry-level learner permit are valid for 18 months.

questions.question: How long must a 16- or 17-year-old hold a learner permit before being eligible for a driver license?
questions.explanation: Drivers age 16 and 17 must hold a learner permit for 6 months.

questions.question: For applicants 19 or older who have never been licensed, how long must they hold a learner permit to waive the driver education requirement?
questions.explanation: Applicants 19+ may be licensed without driver education if they hold a learner permit for 90 days and complete 40 hours of driving.

questions.question: At age 18 there is no learner-permit holding period, but the applicant must complete an approved driver education course.
questions.explanation: Age 18 has no holding period, but an approved driver education course is required.

questions.question: While driving on a learner permit, who must occupy the seat beside you?
choices: A licensed parent, guardian, driving instructor, or responsible adult who signed for financial responsibility
questions.explanation: The person beside a permit driver must be a licensed parent, guardian, approved instructor, or responsible adult who signed for financial responsibility.

questions.question: How many total hours of supervised driving (including 10 after sunset) must a 15–18 year old complete?
questions.explanation: Students must complete 40 hours of driving, 10 of which are after sunset, with a parent or legal guardian.

questions.question: A visitor with a valid out-of-state or out-of-country driver license may drive in Utah for up to one year.
questions.explanation: False — visitors may drive on a valid out-of-state/country license for up to six months, not a year.

questions.question: A driver holding only an out-of-state learner permit may legally drive in Utah.
questions.explanation: Drivers with an out-of-state learner permit may NOT drive in Utah.

questions.question: You may NOT have a Utah driver license if:
choices: You are physically or mentally unable to drive safely
You are not a resident of the state
You have not passed the required examinations
questions.explanation: All of these disqualify you from holding a Utah driver license.

questions.question: By law, you may not possess more than one REAL ID-compliant certificate at a time.
questions.explanation: You may not hold more than one REAL ID-compliant certificate simultaneously (e.g., a Utah license and a Colorado ID).

questions.question: A person is generally considered a Utah resident if they remain in the state for how long during a calendar year?
questions.explanation: Remaining in the state for six months or more during any calendar year makes a person a resident.

questions.question: Even if your driving privilege has been suspended in another state, you are still eligible to obtain a Utah driver license.
questions.explanation: You may not have a Utah license if your driving privilege is denied, suspended, revoked, or disqualified in Utah or any other state.

questions.question: At what age can you get a driver license in Utah without completing a driver education course?
questions.explanation: Applicants 19 years of age or older may be licensed without driver education if they meet the permit-holding and driving-hours requirements.

questions.question: Utah accepts driver education completed in other states, provided it meets Utah's specific requirements.
questions.explanation: Out-of-state driver education is accepted if it meets Utah's required hours (online/home-study out-of-state certificates are not accepted).

questions.question: The 6-month renewal learner permit requires a minimum age of:
questions.explanation: The 6-month learner permit (renewal permit) requires a minimum age of 16.

questions.question: An eye (vision) test is a mandatory requirement when applying for a learner permit.
questions.explanation: Applicants must pass both a written knowledge test and an eye test to get a learner permit.

questions.question: States that are NOT members of the Driver License Compact include:
choices: Georgia, Massachusetts, Wisconsin, Michigan, and Tennessee
questions.explanation: Georgia, Massachusetts, Wisconsin, Michigan, and Tennessee are not members of the Driver License Compact.

questions.question: The entry-level learner permit is valid for 24 months.
questions.explanation: False — the entry-level learner permit is valid for 18 months.

questions.question: Required classroom driver education can be satisfied by which of these?
choices: 27 hours of high school classroom instruction
questions.explanation: Classroom instruction can be 27 hrs (high school), 18 hrs (commercial), or 30 hrs (approved online/home study).

questions.question: Which class of driver license is required for operating a personal vehicle weighing up to 26,000 lbs?
questions.explanation: A Class D license allows you to drive all personal vehicles up to 26,000 pounds.

questions.question: To drive a motorcycle you must have an M endorsement on your license.
questions.explanation: Operating a motorcycle on Utah highways requires a motorcycle (M) endorsement.

questions.question: Which document gives you the privilege to drive a motor vehicle?
questions.explanation: A driver license is the evidence of the privilege to drive a motor vehicle.

questions.question: A provisional Class D license is issued to applicants who are:
questions.explanation: Applicants younger than 21 are issued a provisional Class D license, used for a separate point system.

questions.question: How soon must you notify the Driver License Division after changing your address?
questions.explanation: You must notify the division of an address change within 10 days of moving.

questions.question: Utah law allows you to hold both a driver license certificate and an identification card at the same time.
questions.explanation: Utah law does NOT allow holding a driver license certificate and an identification card at the same time.

questions.question: A provisional license (issued to applicants under 21) expires on the applicant's birthday how many years after issue?
questions.explanation: Provisional licenses expire on the applicant's birthday five years after issue; regular licenses expire after eight years.

questions.question: Which endorsement code stands for Motorcycle?
questions.explanation: M = Motorcycle. (H = Hazardous Materials, N = Tanker, S = School Bus.)

questions.question: Faxed or photocopied identity documents are accepted when applying for a driver license.
questions.explanation: All documents must be original or certified copies; faxed or photocopied documents will NOT be accepted.

questions.question: How many acceptable documents proving your Utah residence address must you provide?
questions.explanation: You must provide two acceptable documents as evidence of your Utah residence address, dated within 90 days.

questions.question: A Driving Privilege Card (DPC) may be used for:
questions.explanation: The DPC may be used for driving purposes only; it is not valid identification or age verification.

questions.question: Which restriction code means the driver must wear corrective lenses?
questions.explanation: Restriction B means the driver must wear corrective lenses. (G = daylight hours only.)

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a Class D driver license?
questions.explanation: The Class D license has a minimum age requirement of 16.

questions.question: Residency and mailing-address documents must be dated within 90 days and display your name and address.
questions.explanation: Residency documents must display your name and address and be dated within 90 days.

questions.question: A Class C license is required to operate a single vehicle under 26,001 lbs used to transport:
choices: 16 or more occupants, including the driver
questions.explanation: Class C covers single vehicles under 26,001 lbs transporting 16+ occupants (including the driver) or placarded hazardous materials; minimum age 21.

questions.question: Your out-of-state driver license must be surrendered when you apply for a Utah driver license.
questions.explanation: Your out-of-state license must be surrendered upon application for a Utah driver license.

questions.question: Under Utah's good driver program, you are eligible for online renewal if you have NOT received more than how many reportable violations in the past eight years?
questions.explanation: You qualify for the good driver program if you have not received more than six reportable violations in the past eight years.

questions.question: A regular (21 and over) Class D license normally expires on your birthday how many years after issue?
questions.explanation: Regular licenses for those over 21 expire on the birthday eight years after issue.

questions.question: Endorsement code 'S' stands for:
questions.explanation: S = School Bus endorsement.

questions.question: A Class C license covers a combined vehicle gross weight rating of 26,001 lbs or more.
questions.explanation: False — that describes a Class A license; Class C is for single vehicles under 26,001 lbs (e.g., 16+ occupants or placarded hazmat).

questions.question: What vision standard must you meet to pass the Utah vision test?
choices: 20/40 vision and 90° peripheral field in at least one eye
questions.explanation: The division requires 20/40 vision and 90° peripheral fields in at least one eye.

questions.question: Drivers age 65 and older must pass a vision test only on their very first license application.
questions.explanation: False — drivers 65 and older must pass a vision test with every license application.

questions.question: A physician has the authority to directly restrict a person's driving privilege.
questions.explanation: A physician cannot restrict driving but is responsible for reporting accurately about a patient's health status.

questions.question: If you fail to meet the vision standard with or without glasses, you must get a statement from your eye doctor before your application can be completed.
questions.explanation: Failing the standard requires a statement from your eye doctor indicating vision conditions and any suggested restrictions.

questions.question: If you have a health condition that may affect your ability to drive safely, you are responsible for:
choices: Reporting it to the Driver License Division and seeking medical advice
questions.explanation: Individuals are personally responsible for reporting health conditions and seeking competent medical evaluation.

questions.question: A review examination always results in losing your driving privilege.
questions.explanation: A review examination does not necessarily lead to withdrawal; drivers are often allowed to drive under applicable restrictions if deemed safe.

questions.question: If you are angry or excited before driving, the handbook recommends you:
choices: Drive faster to get it over with
Give yourself time to cool off and stay off the road
Turn up the radio and speed
questions.explanation: If angry or excited, give yourself time to cool off and stay off the road until you have calmed down.

questions.question: Many medications can cause drowsiness, so a physician may advise against driving until a suitable dosage schedule is worked out.
questions.explanation: Medications may cause drowsiness; a physician may advise against driving until a safe dosage schedule is established.

questions.question: What is the minimum score required to pass the Utah written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must pass the written knowledge test with a score of 80% or better.

questions.question: When taking the driving skills test at the Driver License Division, the DLD provides the vehicle for you.
questions.explanation: False — you must provide your own vehicle, and it must be registered and safe with working safety belts.

questions.question: How many written knowledge tests may you take per day?
questions.explanation: You can take two written knowledge tests per day if the division can facilitate testing.

questions.question: On the driving skills test, you will be scored on:
questions.explanation: You are scored on behavior/attitude, lane changes, parking (including parallel), turns, and overall safe operation.

questions.question: Which action would typically NOT result in an automatic failure on the driving skills test?
choices: Failure to yield to a pedestrian
The vehicle going up onto the sidewalk
The examiner having to take control of the vehicle
questions.explanation: Touching the curb for placement is not listed as an automatic failure, unlike the other dangerous actions.

questions.question: Third-party testers are available to administer the driving skills test.
questions.explanation: Certified third-party testers can administer the driving skills test (a separate fee applies).

questions.question: Only which people are allowed in the vehicle during the driving skills test?
choices: The applicant and the examiner only
questions.explanation: Only the applicant and the examiner are allowed in the vehicle during the driving skills test.

questions.question: You must be at least what age to take a driving skills test at the DLD?
questions.explanation: You must be at least 16 to take a driving skills test at the DLD (15 with a third-party tester or high school).

questions.question: The written knowledge test is timed and must be completed in 15 minutes.
questions.explanation: The written knowledge test is NOT timed, but it generally takes 30 to 45 minutes.

questions.question: The fee allows for how many attempts to pass the written knowledge test within 12 months?
questions.explanation: The fee allows for up to three attempts within 12 months; after that you pay again for three more.

questions.question: On the driving skills test, driving too fast or too slow has no effect on your score.
questions.explanation: False — driving too fast or too slow is among the dangerous actions that cause automatic failure.

questions.question: When taking your driving skills test, you may use parking assist and cruise control.
questions.explanation: You cannot use certain safety features such as parking assist and cruise control during the driving skills test.

questions.question: Everyone in your vehicle must have a fastened safety belt or be in a child seat.
questions.explanation: Everyone in the vehicle must have a fastened safety belt or be in an appropriate child seat.

questions.question: Children 12 and under should ride buckled up in the front passenger seat.
questions.explanation: Children 12 and younger should continue to ride in the BACK seat, which is safest.

questions.question: Children age eight and under must be in an approved child safety seat unless they are at least how tall?
questions.explanation: Children 8 and under must be in an approved child safety seat unless they are 57 inches tall.

questions.question: Children must remain rear-facing in the back seat until at least:
questions.explanation: Children must be rear-facing until at least two years of age and 30 pounds (or longer if the seat allows).

questions.question: How much space should you leave between your chest and the steering wheel?
questions.explanation: You should leave about 10 inches between your chest and the steering wheel.

questions.question: An air bag deploys out of the dashboard at speeds up to:
questions.explanation: An air bag can come out of the dashboard at up to 200 mph — faster than the blink of an eye.

questions.question: You can receive a citation if you or any passenger in your vehicle is not properly restrained.
questions.explanation: You can be cited for each unrestrained passenger; the fine may be waived after a 30-minute safety belt course.

questions.question: Infants may safely ride in the front seat of a vehicle that has a passenger-side air bag.
questions.explanation: Infants should NEVER ride in the front seat of a vehicle with a passenger-side air bag.

questions.question: Children must remain in a forward-facing harness in the back seat until at least:
questions.explanation: Children must use a forward-facing harness in the back seat until at least four years of age and 40 pounds.

questions.question: It is legal to smoke in a vehicle while transporting children 15 years of age or younger.
questions.explanation: False — being stopped for smoking in a vehicle with children 15 or younger is an infraction.

questions.question: Your chances of being killed in a crash are about how many times greater if you are thrown from the vehicle?
questions.explanation: Your chances of being killed are 23 times greater if you are thrown from a car.

questions.question: Disabling or rendering your vehicle's air bag system inoperable is a Class B misdemeanor.
questions.explanation: It is a Class B misdemeanor to remove, fail to repair, or modify your air bag to render it inoperable.

questions.question: When adjusting your seat, the top of the steering wheel should be:
questions.explanation: The top of the steering wheel should be no higher than your shoulders and below chin level.

questions.question: Because most crashes happen at high speed, you don't really need a safety belt for short, low-speed trips.
questions.explanation: False — about 70% of crashes occur under 40 mph, so a safety belt should be worn at any speed.

questions.question: How long before turning or changing lanes must you signal?
choices: Only if other cars are present
questions.explanation: Signals are required for at least two seconds before turning or beginning a lane change.

questions.question: In a construction zone where two lanes merge, both lanes should be used until the merge point. This is called the:
questions.explanation: The zipper method alternates vehicles at the merge point after using both lanes up to it.

questions.question: It is legal to back up on a freeway or interstate if you miss your exit.
questions.explanation: Do NOT back up on a freeway or interstate.

questions.question: When parking next to a curb, the wheels of your car must be no farther than how many inches from the curb?
questions.explanation: When parking next to a curb, your wheel must be no farther than 12 inches from the curb.

questions.question: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant.

questions.question: You may not park within how many feet of a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: You may not park on a crosswalk or within 20 feet of a crosswalk.

questions.question: When passing on a multiple-lane road, you must:
choices: Use your signal before moving your vehicle
Move your vehicle completely into the lane
Adjust your speed to pass the other vehicle
questions.explanation: Passing requires signaling, moving completely into the lane, and adjusting your speed.

questions.question: It is legal to pass another vehicle by driving on the shoulder of the road.
questions.explanation: Never pass on the shoulder.

questions.question: When stopping at a railroad crossing, you must stop how far from the nearest rail?
questions.explanation: Stop more than 15 feet but not more than 50 feet from the nearest rail.

questions.question: On a two-lane roadway, only the traffic behind a school bus must stop for its flashing red lights.
questions.explanation: False — on a two-lane roadway, traffic in BOTH directions must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights.

questions.question: On a divided highway with a median, who must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights?
choices: Only the vehicles behind the bus (same direction)
questions.explanation: On a divided highway with a median, only vehicles behind the bus (same direction) must stop; opposite-direction traffic need not.

questions.question: When passing, you should return to the right-hand lane no less than how far from oncoming vehicles?
questions.explanation: Return to the right-hand lane no less than 200 feet from oncoming vehicles.

questions.question: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Downhill, turn your wheels toward the curb so the front tire rests against it.

questions.question: A three-point turn should only be used:
choices: On a two-lane road too narrow for a U-turn
questions.explanation: Use a three-point turn only when a road is too narrow for a U-turn, and only on a two-lane road.

questions.question: You should make a complete stop at a flashing red light.
questions.explanation: A flashing red light requires a complete stop before proceeding.

questions.question: Where should you finish a left turn on a multiple-lane road?
choices: In the left-most lane (just right of the centerline)
questions.explanation: Finish a left turn in the left-most lane, the one directly to the right of the centerline.

questions.question: It is against the law to cross over or drive in the 'gore area.'
questions.explanation: The gore area (between the solid white lines of a traffic lane and an entrance/exit lane) may not be crossed or driven in.

questions.question: When making a lane change, after checking your mirrors you should:
choices: Check your blind spot by turning your head over your shoulder
questions.explanation: Check your blind spot with a head check over your shoulder before changing lanes.

questions.question: On a flex lane, a red 'X' above the lane means:
questions.explanation: A red X means you cannot use the lane; a green arrow means you can.

questions.question: What is the minimum following distance you should maintain behind the vehicle ahead?
questions.explanation: Maintain at least a two-second following distance; increase it in adverse conditions.

questions.question: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a business or residential area is:
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in a business or residential area is 25 mph.

questions.question: Unless posted otherwise, the speed limit when passing a school during recess or when children are present is:
questions.explanation: The school-zone default is 20 mph when children are going to/from school or when flashing lights operate.

questions.question: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: Speed up to beat the red
The light is about to change to red
questions.explanation: A yellow light means the light is about to change to red.

questions.question: At a flashing yellow arrow, you may:
choices: Turn after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow arrow allows turns after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.question: At a red light you may turn right after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it.
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after coming to a complete stop unless posted otherwise.

questions.question: If you approach an intersection where the traffic lights are not working, you must:
choices: Treat it as a green light
Come to a complete stop and yield to any vehicle on the right
questions.explanation: When signals are out, come to a complete stop and yield the right-of-way to any vehicle on the right.

questions.question: White lane markings separate traffic moving in opposite directions.
questions.explanation: False — yellow markings divide opposite-direction traffic; white markings separate lanes moving the same direction.

questions.question: A dashed yellow line on your side of the road means:
questions.explanation: A dashed yellow line on your side means passing is permitted when it is safe.

questions.question: You must yield to a pedestrian in a crosswalk even if the crosswalk is not marked.
questions.explanation: You must yield to pedestrians entering or in a crosswalk, even if it is not marked.

questions.question: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: Yield to the driver on your right at a four-way stop if you arrive at the same time.

questions.question: The paved area of a freeway outside the solid white line (the shoulder/emergency lane) may be used:
questions.explanation: The area outside the solid white line is for emergency use only; passing or stopping for passengers there is illegal.

questions.question: A traffic roundabout is a circular intersection in which traffic flows clockwise.
questions.explanation: False — roundabout traffic flows counterclockwise.

questions.question: To use the I-15 express (carpool) lane for free, your vehicle generally must carry:
questions.explanation: Express lanes may be used free by vehicles with two or more persons (also motorcycles, buses, and C-decal clean-fuel vehicles).

questions.question: It is illegal to cross a double white line, such as the line separating the express lane.
questions.explanation: Crossing a double white line is illegal; move in and out of the carpool lane only at marked access points.

questions.question: Warning signs are typically what shape and color?
questions.explanation: Warning signs are usually diamond-shaped and yellow with black symbols.

questions.question: Regardless of the traffic light, you must always obey:
choices: The instructions of a police officer at an intersection
questions.explanation: Always obey a police officer's instructions at an intersection, even over the traffic light.

questions.question: When using a shared center left-turn lane, you may not enter it more than how far before making your turn?
questions.explanation: You may not enter a shared center turn lane more than 500 feet before making the turn.

questions.question: Guide signs (directing you to cities, services, and points of interest) are typically:
questions.explanation: Guide signs are square or rectangular and green, brown, or blue.

questions.question: When merging into a lane of traffic, you must yield to vehicles already in the continuing traffic lane.
questions.explanation: When merging, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles in the continuing lane that are an immediate threat.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
questions.explanation: A red octagon always means STOP — come to a complete stop.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Slow down and yield to traffic that has the right-of-way
questions.explanation: A yield sign means slow down and yield to traffic that has the right-of-way.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
questions.explanation: This regulatory sign means Do Not Enter — do not proceed into this roadway.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
questions.explanation: A turn symbol inside a red circle with a slash means that movement is prohibited — here, no U-turn.

questions.question: What does this round yellow sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing ahead — slow down, look, and listen
questions.explanation: A round yellow sign with an X and 'RR' warns of a railroad crossing ahead.

questions.question: What does this white X-shaped sign mean?
questions.explanation: The crossbuck marks the railroad crossing itself; you must yield to train traffic and stop if a train is coming.

questions.question: An orange diamond sign like this indicates:
questions.explanation: Orange signs with black symbols warn of highway work zones; slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.question: This fluorescent yellow-green pentagon sign warns of:
questions.explanation: The five-sided fluorescent yellow-green sign marks a school zone or school crossing.

questions.question: This yellow pennant-shaped sign means:
questions.explanation: The pennant-shaped sign on the left side of the road marks a no-passing zone.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: You are going the wrong way — turn around
questions.explanation: A WRONG WAY sign means you are traveling against traffic and must turn around.

questions.question: This white rectangular sign is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Speed limit signs are regulatory signs — white with black lettering — and you must obey them.

questions.question: What does this sign warn you about?
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates a pedestrian crossing ahead.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Bicycle crossing / share the road with bicycles
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates a bicycle crossing — watch for and share the road with bicyclists.

questions.question: What does this sign warn about?
questions.explanation: The car-with-skid-marks symbol warns that the road is slippery when wet.

questions.question: What does this yellow diamond sign mean?
choices: A curve in the road ahead
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates a curve ahead; slow down as needed.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic moves only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: A one-way sign means traffic moves only in the direction of the arrow; never drive against it.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
questions.explanation: A left-turn arrow inside a red circle with a slash means no left turn.

questions.question: What does this yellow diamond sign warn of?
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates a T-intersection ahead — the road you are on ends.

questions.question: What does this yellow diamond sign mean?
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates merging traffic ahead.

questions.question: What does this yellow diamond sign indicate?
questions.explanation: This warning sign indicates a divided highway begins ahead.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
questions.explanation: A right-turn arrow inside a red circle with a slash means no right turn.

questions.question: For a driver 21 or older, it is illegal to drive with a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of:
questions.explanation: In Utah, you violate the law if your BAC is over 0.05 (0.04 for CDL holders).

questions.question: Alcohol and other impairing drugs reduce which of the following?
questions.explanation: Alcohol and drugs reduce judgment, vision, color distinction, and reaction time.

questions.question: If you are under 21 and drive with any measurable amount of alcohol in your body, your driving privileges will be denied for (first offense):
questions.explanation: Under the Not-a-Drop law, a first offense results in denial of driving privileges for six months.

questions.question: If you are under 21 and convicted of a first DUI, you are restricted to driving a vehicle with an ignition interlock device for:
questions.explanation: A driver under 21 convicted of a first DUI is restricted to an ignition interlock device for three years.

questions.question: At what BAC is a driver's vision impacted, according to the handbook?
questions.explanation: Vision is impacted at 0.02 BAC for all drivers; vision is the most important sense used in driving.

questions.question: Refusing a chemical test under the implied consent law results in only a 90-day license suspension for a first offense.
questions.explanation: False — refusing a chemical test revokes your license for 18 months (first offense) and 36 months for a subsequent offense.

questions.question: On a first DUI conviction for a driver 21 and older, the driver license is suspended for:
questions.explanation: A first DUI for a driver 21 and older results in a 120-day suspension.

questions.question: Alcohol and other impairing drugs are involved in only about 10% of fatal traffic crashes each year.
questions.explanation: False — they are involved in approximately 40% of fatal traffic crashes each year.

questions.question: Alcohol consumption has no effect on over-the-counter or prescription medication.
questions.explanation: Alcohol can have an unpredictable effect on drugs; they should not be taken at the same time.

questions.question: Operating a motorboat while under the influence is subject to the same laws as driving a motor vehicle while under the influence.
questions.explanation: Boating under the influence is treated under the same laws as a DUI in a motor vehicle.

questions.question: Impairment from alcohol starts only after several drinks.
questions.explanation: Impairment starts with the first drink; even one drink can affect your ability to operate a vehicle.

questions.question: How often must an ignition interlock device be monitored?
questions.explanation: Ignition interlock-restricted drivers must have the device monitored at least every 60 days.

questions.question: If your license requires an ignition interlock device but you drive a car without one, your license could be taken away.
questions.explanation: Driving without a required interlock device can lead to impound and revocation of your license for one year.

questions.question: The most important sense you use while driving is:
questions.explanation: Vision is the most important sense used in driving, and it is the first impaired by alcohol.

questions.question: Any activity that diverts your mental or physical attention from driving is considered distracted driving.
questions.explanation: Distracted driving is any activity that takes your mind or body away from the driving task.

questions.question: Committing two or more moving violations within a single, continuous period of driving is considered careless driving.
questions.explanation: Careless driving includes committing two or more moving violations within a single continuous period of driving.

questions.question: Which is the BEST practice before taking a road trip?
choices: Do not drink alcohol and drive
Plan to stop every two hours to take a break
questions.explanation: Good trip preparation includes adequate sleep, not drinking and driving, and stopping every two hours.

questions.question: Drivers under the age of 18 may use a handheld wireless device while driving only to:
choices: Handle a medical emergency, report a hazard/crime, or contact a parent/guardian
questions.explanation: Under-18 drivers may use a handheld device only during a medical emergency, to report a safety hazard or crime, or to contact a parent/guardian.

questions.question: Most passenger cars will begin to lose control or hydroplane at speeds over:
questions.explanation: Your car may start to hydroplane at speeds over 35 mph when front tires ride on water instead of the road.

questions.question: If your rear wheels skid to the right, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Steer into the skid — if the rear wheels skid right, turn the front wheels right (without oversteering).

questions.question: If you see a flooded roadway ahead, you should turn around and find another route.
questions.explanation: Do not drive through flooded areas — turn around and find another route.

questions.question: How much water can carry away most vehicles, including SUVs and pickups?
questions.explanation: Two feet of moving water can carry away most vehicles; six inches reaches the bottom of most cars.

questions.question: Carrying extra water for your vehicle's radiator is a sensible practice when driving through the desert.
questions.explanation: Carry water for both yourself and your vehicle's radiator when driving in the desert.

questions.question: If your vehicle breaks down in the desert, you should:
choices: Walk away to find help immediately
questions.explanation: If your vehicle breaks down in the desert, stay with it unless help is within a few minutes' walk.

questions.question: At night, you must dim your high beams within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming car (and within 300 feet when following another vehicle).

questions.question: When following another vehicle at night, you should dim your high beams within how many feet?
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams when you are within 300 feet of the vehicle ahead of you.

questions.question: On a narrow mountain road, a vehicle traveling uphill must yield to vehicles going downhill.
questions.explanation: False — it is the opposite: a vehicle going downhill must yield to vehicles going uphill on a narrow road.

questions.question: Coasting downhill in neutral or with the clutch disengaged is a recommended mountain-driving practice.
questions.explanation: Do NOT coast downhill in neutral or with the clutch disengaged; use a low gear.

questions.question: If your vehicle enters water, it will generally float on the surface for about:
questions.explanation: A vehicle in water floats only about 30 to 60 seconds, so attempt to get out immediately.

questions.question: Most people need how many hours of sleep to stay awake during the day?
questions.explanation: Most people need 7 to 9 hours of sleep to stay awake during the day.

questions.question: During a lightning storm, a hard-topped vehicle with windows up offers protection, and you should not touch any metal surfaces.
questions.explanation: Only a hard-topped vehicle protects you in a lightning storm; keep windows up and avoid touching metal.

questions.question: If you become tired while driving, the best response is to:
choices: Pull off at a rest area for a 20-minute power nap or sleep
Open the window and drive faster
questions.explanation: If you feel tired, stop driving and take a 20-minute power nap or find a place to sleep.

questions.question: A conviction for a texting-while-driving violation is generally a:
choices: Class C misdemeanor with a maximum $100 fine
questions.explanation: A texting violation is a Class C misdemeanor (max $100), or a Class B misdemeanor if it causes injury or is a repeat offense.

questions.question: A road rage conviction can never affect your driver license.
questions.explanation: False — the court may order your license suspended for one year on a first road rage conviction.

questions.question: You must notify law enforcement (911) if a crash results in injury, death, or property damage of at least:
questions.explanation: Notify 911 immediately if a crash causes injury, death, or at least $2,500 in total property damage.

questions.question: What information must be exchanged between drivers after a crash?
questions.explanation: Drivers must exchange name and address, vehicle registration number, and insurance information.

questions.question: Failing to stop at the scene of an injury crash is only a minor infraction.
questions.explanation: False — it is a third-degree felony to fail to stop at the scene of an injury crash.

questions.question: If your brakes stop working while driving, you should first:
choices: Use the parking brake, applying it slowly
questions.explanation: If your brakes fail, use the parking brake, applying it slowly to avoid locking the rear wheels.

questions.question: To use anti-lock brakes (ABS) correctly in an emergency stop, you should:
choices: Press the brake pedal firmly and hold steady pressure
questions.explanation: With ABS, press the brake pedal hard and hold steady pressure; do not pump. You can still steer.

questions.question: If a front tire blows out, you should:
choices: Grip the wheel firmly, steer straight, ease off the gas, and slow gradually
questions.explanation: In a blowout, do not slam the brakes — grip the wheel, steer straight, take your foot off the gas, and slow gradually.

questions.question: If your gas pedal sticks and the vehicle speeds out of control, you should:
choices: Shift to neutral and look for a safe place to pull off
questions.explanation: If the gas pedal sticks, shift to neutral, then steer smoothly and brake gently to pull off the road.

questions.question: Driving without owner's security (insurance) is a class B misdemeanor that may cost you your license and registration.
questions.explanation: Operating without required insurance is a class B misdemeanor and can cost your license, registration, and plates.

questions.question: If your vehicle breaks down, you should place emergency flares or triangles how far behind your vehicle?
questions.explanation: Place flares or triangles 200 to 300 feet behind your vehicle to give other drivers time to react.

questions.question: SR22 'future proof' of financial responsibility must generally be maintained for:
questions.explanation: SR22 proof must be maintained for three years following the suspension/revocation (or reinstatement for an unsatisfied judgment).

questions.question: After a crash you are required to render reasonable assistance to anyone who is injured.
questions.explanation: You must render reasonable assistance, including arranging transport for injured persons who need treatment.

questions.question: Which insurance coverage is mandatory in Utah?
choices: Bodily injury/property damage liability and Personal Injury Protection (PIP)
questions.explanation: Liability (bodily injury/property damage) and Personal Injury Protection (no-fault/PIP) are mandatory; collision and comprehensive are not required by law.

questions.question: Defensive driving means looking for possible crashes and mistakes other drivers may make.
questions.explanation: Defensive driving involves anticipating possible crashes and the mistakes of other drivers.

questions.question: How long do alcohol/drug-related driving violations, suspensions, and revocations stay on your driving record?
questions.explanation: Alcohol/drug-related violations, suspensions, and revocations stay on your record for 10 years.

questions.question: Moving traffic violation convictions generally remain on your driving record for:
questions.explanation: The record contains convictions for moving violations and suspensions within the past three years.

questions.question: A driver UNDER 21 will be asked to appear for a hearing after accumulating how many points within three years?
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 are called to a hearing at 70 or more points within a three-year period (200 for drivers over 21).

questions.question: A driver OVER 21 will be asked to appear for a hearing after accumulating how many points within three years?
questions.explanation: Drivers over 21 are called to a hearing at 200 or more points within a three-year period.

questions.question: How many points does reckless driving carry under Utah's point system?
questions.explanation: Reckless driving carries 80 points, the highest single value listed.

questions.question: If you drive while your license is denied, suspended, or revoked, there is no possible jail penalty.
questions.explanation: False — you may be sentenced to up to 90 days in jail, and your suspension is extended.

questions.question: Half of the total points on your record are removed after you drive one full year without a moving-violation conviction.
questions.explanation: Half your points are removed after one conviction-free year; all are removed after two successive years.

questions.question: Completing a recommended defensive driving course can remove how many points from your record (once per three years)?
questions.explanation: Up to 50 points may be removed once per three-year period for completing an approved defensive driving course.

questions.question: Points for an individual conviction are automatically removed from your record after:
questions.explanation: Points for individual convictions are automatically removed three years after the date of the violation.

questions.question: It is unlawful to display a license you know has been canceled, suspended, or altered.
questions.explanation: Displaying a canceled, suspended, or altered license is unlawful and can result in suspension.

questions.question: Reckless driving is defined as operating a vehicle with:
choices: Willful and/or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property
questions.explanation: Reckless driving is operating a vehicle in willful and/or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property.

questions.question: A second or subsequent DUI conviction results only in a license suspension, not a revocation.
questions.explanation: False — a second or subsequent DUI conviction results in a mandatory license revocation.

questions.question: The blind-spot area surrounding larger vehicles is commonly referred to as a 'no-zone.'
questions.explanation: The 'no-zone' is the area of a large vehicle's blind spots — front, sides, and rear.

questions.question: What is a blind spot?
choices: The area of roadway you can see with mirrors
The area of roadway that can't be seen with mirrors while operating a vehicle
The area more than 1,500 ft ahead of you
The area within 500 ft at night
questions.explanation: A blind spot is the area of roadway that can't be seen with mirrors while operating a vehicle.

questions.question: Utah law requires you to leave at least how much distance when passing a bicyclist?
questions.explanation: Utah law requires a minimum of three feet of distance when passing a bicyclist or other vulnerable user.

questions.question: When approaching a stationary emergency vehicle with flashing lights, you should slow down, move over if safe, and give it as much space as possible.
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires you to slow down, change lanes if safe, and provide as much space as possible.

questions.question: When an emergency vehicle approaches using sirens and lights, you must:
choices: Speed up to clear the area
Drive to the right side of the road and stop until it passes
Stop in the middle of the lane
questions.explanation: Yield to emergency vehicles by driving to the right side of the road and stopping until it passes.

questions.question: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should:
questions.explanation: Give motorcycles an entire lane; do not share the lane, as they need room to react.

questions.question: If you can't see a truck driver in their side mirror, the driver can still see you just fine.
questions.explanation: False — if you can't see the truck driver in their side mirror, they can't see you either.

questions.question: A large truck's blind spot (no-zone) at the rear can extend up to:
questions.explanation: Large vehicles have huge rear blind spots up to 200 feet behind them.

questions.question: When a pedestrian is crossing with a guide dog or a white cane, you must come to a complete stop.
questions.explanation: Always yield to the visually impaired; when they cross with a guide dog or white cane, you must come to a complete stop.

questions.question: When the police pull you over, you should:
choices: Move to the right side of the road (even from an HOV lane) and stop in a safe spot
questions.explanation: Pull over to the right side of the road, even from an HOV lane, and stop in a safe spot.

questions.question: During a traffic stop, you should keep your hands:
questions.explanation: Stay calm, keep your safety belt fastened, and keep your hands on the steering wheel.

questions.question: An orange-and-red reflective triangle on the rear of a vehicle means the vehicle:
choices: Is moving less than 25 mph (slow-moving vehicle)
questions.explanation: The slow-moving vehicle emblem indicates a vehicle moving less than 25 mph.

questions.question: At railroad crossings, cars have the right-of-way over trains.
questions.explanation: False — trains have the right-of-way 100% of the time.

questions.question: It is permitted to drive around a railroad crossing gate as long as you go slowly.
questions.explanation: Never drive through, around, or under a crossing gate while it is down or being raised/lowered.

questions.question: Lane filtering by a motorcycle is allowed only when the road's speed limit is:
questions.explanation: Lane filtering is allowed on roads with a speed limit of 45 mph or less, with the motorcycle going 15 mph or less and the other vehicle stopped.

questions.question: Bicycles are considered vehicles and must follow the same rules of the road as motor vehicles.
questions.explanation: Bicycles are considered vehicles on roadways and must follow the same rules of the road.

questions.question: Unless on official business, you may not follow a fire vehicle responding to an alarm closer than:
questions.explanation: Do not follow within 500 feet of any fire vehicle responding to an alarm.

questions.question: Any motorcycle operator or passenger under what age must wear an approved DOT helmet?
questions.explanation: Any motorcycle operator or passenger under 21 must wear an approved DOT helmet.

questions.question: A bicyclist approaching a stop sign may proceed through without a full stop if they slow to a reasonable speed and yield to pedestrians and traffic.
questions.explanation: Bicyclists may treat a stop sign as a yield — slowing and yielding rather than fully stopping.

questions.question: Why do large trucks often swing wide to the left before making a right turn?
choices: Because the rear wheels follow a shorter path than the front wheels
It is illegal and a mistake
questions.explanation: On a long vehicle the rear wheels track a shorter path, so the driver must swing wide to complete a right turn.

questions.question: With regular hitches and couplings, no safety chain or cable is needed when towing a vehicle.
questions.explanation: False — a chain or cable strong enough to prevent separation is required, even with regular hitches.

questions.question: A motor vehicle's horn must be able to be heard under normal conditions from a distance of at least:
questions.explanation: All motor vehicles must have one functioning horn audible from no less than 200 feet.

questions.question: Headlights must be white and shine how far on high beam?
questions.explanation: Headlights must be white and shine 350 feet on high beam and 100 feet on low beam.

questions.question: The drawbar or connection between towed vehicles may not exceed:
questions.explanation: The connection between towing vehicles may not exceed 15 feet in length.

questions.question: When a tow connection is a chain, rope, or cable, you must attach a red flag at least:
questions.explanation: A red flag at least 12 inches (one foot) square must be attached to a chain, rope, or cable tow connection.

questions.question: Studded snow tires may be used in Utah year-round.
questions.explanation: False — studded snow tires may be used only from October 15 through March 31.

questions.question: A windshield is required on all vehicles except:
questions.explanation: A windshield is required on all vehicles except motorcycles, and it must be clear of anything that obstructs vision.

questions.question: The maximum legal height of a vehicle, including any load, is:
questions.explanation: No vehicle may exceed a height of 14 feet, including any load carried.

questions.question: Any vehicle may be equipped with a siren, whistle, or bell.
questions.explanation: False — only authorized emergency vehicles may have sirens, whistles, or bells.

questions.question: Tail and stop lights must be red and visible from how far to the rear?
questions.explanation: Vehicles must have two red tail/stop lights visible from 500 feet to the rear in normal sunlight.

questions.question: Red or blue lights visible from the front of a vehicle may be used by any driver who wants extra visibility.
questions.explanation: Red or blue front-facing lights may only be used by emergency, school, snow removal, or other authorized vehicles.

questions.question: Warning devices must be displayed when a vehicle 80 inches wide or 30 feet long is disabled on the road for more than:
questions.explanation: Warning devices are required when such a vehicle is disabled or stopped on the road for more than 10 minutes.

questions.question: A vision test is required on all applications processed in a field office.
questions.explanation: A vision test is required on all applications processed in a field office.

questions.question: The Driver License Medical Advisory Board emphasizes:
choices: The functional ability to drive a vehicle safely
questions.explanation: The board emphasizes the functional ability to drive safely rather than stressing impairments.

questions.question: Frequently receiving traffic citations can be a behavioral hint about your mental fitness to drive.
questions.explanation: Frequently getting traffic citations is listed as one behavioral hint about mental health affecting driving.

questions.question: The medical fitness guidelines require higher standards for drivers of commercial vehicles than for private vehicles.
questions.explanation: Guidelines require higher fitness standards for commercial drivers, though the principles are the same.

questions.question: First-time applicants must also pass the Traffic Safety and Trends Exam with a score of:
questions.explanation: All first-time driver license applicants must pass the Traffic Safety and Trends Exam with 100%.

questions.question: About 90% of the Traffic Safety and Trends Exam relates to significant causes of traffic deaths.
questions.explanation: False — about 50% of that exam relates to the leading causes of traffic-related deaths.

questions.question: About how long does the driving skills test last?
questions.explanation: The driving skills test lasts about 20 minutes and consists of basic maneuvers.

questions.question: You may take up to three driving skills tests in a single day.
questions.explanation: False — you may take only one driving skills test per day.

questions.question: The vehicle you bring for the driving skills test must have working safety belts.
questions.explanation: The test vehicle must be registered and comply with safety laws, including functioning safety belts.

questions.question: All driving skills tests must be completed within six months from the day you pay your fee.
questions.explanation: All tests must be completed within six months from the day you pay your fee.

questions.question: How many points does running a red light carry?
questions.explanation: Running a red light carries 50 points (the same as a stop-sign violation).

questions.question: How many points does failure to yield right-of-way carry?
questions.explanation: Failure to yield right-of-way carries 60 points.

questions.question: Following too closely (tailgating) carries only 20 points under Utah's point system.
questions.explanation: False — following too closely carries 60 points.

questions.question: If you drive while suspended for an alcohol-related offense, the fine may be up to $1,000 and you may be jailed up to six months.
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended/revoked for an alcohol offense can bring a fine up to $1,000 and up to six months in jail.

questions.question: Manslaughter or automobile homicide while driving results in a mandatory revocation.
questions.explanation: Manslaughter or automobile homicide while driving is grounds for mandatory revocation.

questions.question: The rear license plate light must be white and make the plate readable from at least:
questions.explanation: A white license plate light must make the plate readable from at least 50 feet.

questions.question: All vehicles with windshields must have at least one functioning windshield wiper.
questions.explanation: All vehicles with windshields must have at least one wiper; if originally equipped with two, both must work.

questions.question: The maximum legal outside width of a vehicle, including its load, is generally:
questions.explanation: No vehicle may have a total outside width, including load, of more than 8 1/2 feet (farm tractors may reach 9 feet).

questions.question: Motorcycles are required to have at least two operational headlights.
questions.explanation: False — all vehicles EXCEPT motorcycles must have at least two headlights; a motorcycle may have one.

questions.question: If your vehicle breaks down on a one-way street, the rear warning flag should be placed how far to the rear?
questions.explanation: On a one-way street, the rear flag should be placed 200 feet to the rear.

questions.question: More than half the drivers killed in two-car crashes in Utah were not at fault.
questions.explanation: Over half the drivers killed in two-car crashes in Utah were not at fault — a key reason to drive defensively.

questions.question: Most crashes happen during which time of day?
questions.explanation: The time of day when most crashes happen is between 2 p.m. and 6 p.m.

questions.question: If a vehicle owner has no insurance, the DMV must revoke the registration unless insurance is purchased within:
questions.explanation: The registration must be revoked unless insurance is purchased within 30 days.

questions.question: If the engine shuts off while driving, you should keep a firm grip on the steering wheel and steer to a safe place to pull off.
questions.explanation: On a power failure, grip the wheel firmly (it will be harder to turn), brake gently, and pull off the road.

questions.question: Your headrest should rest against the base of your neck, below your ears.
questions.explanation: False — the headrest should contact the back of your head above the level of your ears.

questions.question: You may not park within how many feet of a stop sign, yield sign, or traffic control signal?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 30 feet of any flashing beacon, stop sign, yield sign, or traffic control signal.

questions.question: The shoulder of an interstate highway may be used for parking only if your vehicle breaks down or you are in physical distress.
questions.explanation: Interstate shoulders may be used only for breakdowns or physical distress, not ordinary parking.

questions.question: On rural interstate highways, posted speed limits may be as high as:
questions.explanation: Rural interstate speed limits in Utah may be posted at 65, 70, 75, or 80 mph.

questions.question: A flashing red traffic light means:
choices: Stop and look both ways before entering the intersection
The signal is broken — ignore it
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means stop and look both ways before entering the intersection (treat like a stop sign).

questions.question: A fully loaded tractor-trailer traveling at 55 mph may take how far to come to a complete stop?
questions.explanation: A fully loaded tractor-trailer may take more than 400 feet to stop — far longer than a car's ~130-140 feet.

questions.question: If convicted of not following the Move Over law, you must attend a four-hour defensive driving course within 90 days or your license is suspended for 90 days.
questions.explanation: A Move Over conviction requires a four-hour live course within 90 days; failing to attend means a 90-day suspension.

questions.question: If you are under 21, it is illegal to purchase, possess, or drink alcoholic beverages.
questions.explanation: Under 21, it is illegal to purchase, possess, and drink alcoholic beverages, and zero-tolerance laws apply to driving.

questions.question: If an animal is standing near or on the roadway, you should not swerve to avoid it.
questions.explanation: Do not swerve to avoid an animal — keep control and stay in your lane; swerving raises your injury risk.

questions.question: Black ice is dangerous mainly because it:
choices: Is difficult to see and looks more like a wet spot than ice
questions.explanation: Black ice is hard to see and appears more like a wet spot than a patch of ice, causing many crashes.

questions.question: If you become stranded in cold weather, you should stay with your vehicle.
questions.explanation: Stay with your vehicle if stranded in the cold; most deaths happen when people leave and become lost.

questions.question: When stranded in the cold, it is recommended to run the engine and heater about how long each hour?
questions.explanation: Run the engine roughly 10 minutes each hour to charge the battery and warm the interior, as fuel allows.

questions.question: While running the engine in a stranded, snow-covered vehicle, you should keep the exhaust pipe clear of snow.
questions.explanation: Keep the exhaust pipe clear of snow; blocked exhaust can leak fumes into the vehicle and cause blackout or death.

questions.question: Even in freezing temperatures, you should leave at least one window slightly open when running the engine in a stranded vehicle.
questions.explanation: Leave one window open for fresh air to avoid carbon monoxide poisoning when ventilation is inadequate.

questions.question: The fatigue warning sign that you may fall asleep includes:
choices: You can't remember the last few miles driven
You are driving below the limit
questions.explanation: Not remembering the last few miles is a classic drowsiness warning sign — stop and rest.

questions.question: When a snowplow displays flashing yellow lights, you may freely pass it on the side where the plow blade is deployed.
questions.explanation: False — you may not pass or overtake a snowplow on the side where a plow blade is deployed.

questions.question: If three or more snowplows are operating in echelon (staggered) formation, you may not pass them on either side.
questions.explanation: With three or more snowplows in echelon formation, you may not pass or overtake them on either side.

questions.question: You should NEVER pass another vehicle:
choices: When a curve or hill blocks your view
questions.explanation: Never pass at intersections, railroad crossings/bridges, when your view is blocked by a curve or hill, or in a no-passing zone.

questions.question: When backing up, you should put your right arm on the back of the seat and look through the rear window.
questions.explanation: Look through the rear window with your arm over the seat; mirrors do not show the area right behind you.

questions.question: It is legal to make a U-turn on a freeway when traffic is light.
questions.explanation: False — U-turns are never allowed on a freeway.

questions.question: A child who has outgrown a forward-facing harness should remain in a booster seat until the safety belt fits correctly and they are at least:
questions.explanation: Use a booster seat until the safety belt fits correctly and the child is 57 inches tall.

questions.question: The lap portion of a safety belt should rest across your stomach.
questions.explanation: False — the lap belt should fit across your hips, not your stomach.

questions.question: The shoulder harness should never be worn under your arm or behind your back.
questions.explanation: The shoulder harness must not be under your arm or behind your back; it goes across your shoulder and chest.

questions.question: Child car seats must be crash-tested and meet Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards to be approved.
questions.explanation: Child car seats must be crash-tested and meet Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards.

questions.question: To set your left-side mirror, you should rest your head against the closed left window and adjust it so that you:
choices: Cannot see your vehicle at all
Can barely see the rear left edge of your vehicle
questions.explanation: Set the left mirror so you can barely see the rear left edge of your vehicle, maximizing your side/rear view.

questions.question: When adjusting your seat, you should sit straight with your back against the seat and your feet on the floor.
questions.explanation: Sit straight with your back against the seat and feet on the floor, able to reach the pedals.

questions.question: If you are worried about something while driving, the handbook suggests you:
choices: Pull over and stop driving entirely
Do something to help you concentrate, like listening to the radio
questions.explanation: If worried, do something that lets you concentrate on driving, such as listening to the radio.

questions.question: Leaving a few minutes early can keep you from feeling the need to speed or beat traffic signals.
questions.explanation: If you are impatient, give yourself extra time by leaving early so you don't feel pressured to speed.

questions.question: You have the final responsibility for knowing your abilities and for driving safely.
questions.explanation: You bear the final responsibility for knowing your abilities and driving safely.

questions.question: Abuse or excessive use of prescription drugs has caused many serious crashes and should be avoided.
questions.explanation: Abuse or excessive use of prescription drugs has caused many serious accidents and should be avoided.

questions.question: A motorcyclist or bicyclist (16+) stopped at a signal that won't detect them may proceed with caution after waiting at least:
questions.explanation: After a complete stop and waiting a reasonable period of not less than 90 seconds, they may proceed if no other traffic or pedestrians are near.

questions.question: When sharing the road with a horse-drawn vehicle or horseback rider, you should not use your horn or rev your engine.
questions.explanation: Do not use your horn or rev your engine near horses; it may scare the horse and cause a crash.

questions.question: Horse-drawn vehicles and horseback riders have the same right to the road as motor vehicles.
questions.explanation: They have the same right to the road as a motor vehicle and must follow the same rules.

questions.question: Golf cart operators must comply with the same rules as a bicycle and may not consume alcohol while operating.
questions.explanation: Golf cart operators follow the same rules as a bicycle and are prohibited from consuming alcohol while operating.

questions.question: A Class 1 electric-assisted bicycle stops providing motor assistance once it reaches:
questions.explanation: A Class 1 e-bike assists only while pedaling and ceases assistance at 20 mph.

questions.question: A Class 3 electric-assisted bicycle ceases motor assistance at what speed and is equipped with a speedometer?
questions.explanation: A Class 3 e-bike provides pedal assistance up to 28 mph and is equipped with a speedometer.

questions.question: A street-legal ATV/UTV operating on a highway may not exceed:
choices: The lesser of the posted speed limit or 50 mph
The posted limit, even if 70 mph
questions.explanation: A street-legal ATV/UTV may not exceed the lesser of the posted speed limit or 50 mph.

questions.question: At a light rail (TRAX) crossing, you must wait until the gates have fully raised and the red lights stop flashing before entering.
questions.explanation: Treat light rail crossings like any train crossing — wait until gates fully raise and lights stop flashing, and never stop on the tracks.

questions.question: On multi-lane highways, tractor-trailers should stay in the far-left lane.
questions.explanation: False — trucks tend to stay in the center lane to help traffic merge on and off the highway.

questions.question: When passing a tractor-trailer, you should not linger alongside it.
questions.explanation: Don't linger alongside a truck when passing; pass completely and on the left so the trucker can take evasive action if needed.

questions.question: A ramp meter lets as many vehicles as want enter the freeway each time the green light comes on.
questions.explanation: False — a ramp meter typically releases only one vehicle per green light (unless posted otherwise).

questions.question: A ramp meter signal uses which lights?
choices: Only red and green that alternate
questions.explanation: Ramp meters look like traffic signals but use only alternating red and green lights.

questions.question: You may use a runaway-vehicle ramp only in an emergency requiring it to stop your vehicle.
questions.explanation: Runaway-vehicle ramps may be used only in an emergency; stopping, standing, or parking on one otherwise is prohibited.

questions.question: A green arrow traffic signal means:
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow after yielding to vehicles and pedestrians still in the intersection
Yield to oncoming traffic that has a green light
questions.explanation: A green arrow is a protected movement — proceed in the arrow's direction after yielding to anyone still in the intersection.

questions.question: You should not turn into a lane just because an oncoming vehicle has its turn signal on.
questions.explanation: The other driver may plan to turn after passing you, or forgot to cancel the signal — don't assume.

questions.question: Utah has no basic speed law, so you may always drive the posted limit regardless of conditions.
questions.explanation: False — Utah's basic speed law says you may never drive faster than is reasonably safe for conditions.

questions.question: You are required to slow down when approaching a curve, the top of a hill, or a railroad crossing.
questions.explanation: The law requires slowing for curves, hilltops, intersections, railroad crossings, and other hazards.

questions.question: Uninsured motorist coverage protects you when:
choices: You are hit by a driver who has no insurance or by a hit-and-run driver
You cause damage to someone else
questions.explanation: Uninsured motorist coverage covers injuries to you and your passengers from an uninsured or hit-and-run driver (property damage is generally not paid).

questions.question: Comprehensive coverage pays for vehicle damage caused by:
choices: Fire, theft, vandalism, or glass breakage
questions.explanation: Comprehensive covers non-collision damage such as fire, theft, vandalism, or glass breakage.

questions.question: If your wheels drop off the paved road surface onto a lower shoulder, you should:
choices: Jerk the wheel back onto the road
Slow down gradually, then steer back onto the roadway
questions.explanation: Don't panic at a drop-off — slow down gradually, then ease back onto the pavement.

questions.question: Personal Injury Protection (PIP, or 'no-fault') coverage is optional in Utah.
questions.explanation: False — PIP (no-fault) coverage is mandatory in Utah.

questions.question: Depending on severity, a speeding conviction can carry how many points?
questions.explanation: Speeding carries 35–75 points depending on severity.

questions.question: How many points does texting while driving carry?
questions.explanation: Texting while driving carries 50 points.

questions.question: If you drive while suspended, the length of your original suspension is increased by the amount of the original time period.
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended adds the original suspension length again (e.g., a 3-month suspension gains another 3 months).

questions.question: How many points does improper passing carry?
questions.explanation: Improper passing carries 50 points.

questions.question: A load may not extend more than how far beyond the FRONT of a vehicle's body?
questions.explanation: A load may not extend more than three feet to the front of the vehicle's body.

questions.question: A load may not extend more than how far beyond the REAR of a vehicle's body?
questions.explanation: A load may not extend more than six feet to the rear of the vehicle's body.

questions.question: A load may not extend more than six inches beyond the line of the fenders on the right side of the vehicle.
questions.explanation: Loads may not extend beyond the left fender line, nor more than six inches beyond the right fender line.

questions.question: Excluding fire-fighting equipment, the maximum overall length of a single vehicle is:
questions.explanation: A single vehicle may not exceed 45 feet overall (combinations may not exceed 65 feet).

questions.question: A combination of vehicles, such as a motor home pulling a boat, may be up to 80 feet long.
questions.explanation: False — a combination of vehicles may not exceed 65 feet in total length.

questions.question: How many hours of behind-the-wheel instruction does Utah driver education require?
questions.explanation: Driver education requires 6 hours of behind-the-wheel instruction (plus 6 hours of observation).

questions.question: A non-resident licensed by another state or country may drive in Utah if they are at least 16 years of age.
questions.explanation: Non-residents licensed elsewhere may drive in Utah if they are at least 16.

questions.question: If you let your learner permit expire, you must retake the written knowledge test.
questions.explanation: If you renew or let your permit expire, you must retake the written knowledge test.

questions.question: Endorsement code 'P' stands for:
questions.explanation: P = Passenger endorsement.

questions.question: A Driving Privilege Card (DPC) is valid for eight years, like a regular license.
questions.explanation: False — a DPC expires each year on the applicant's birthday.

questions.question: Restriction code 'G' on a license means the driver is restricted to:
questions.explanation: Restriction G limits driving to daylight hours only.

questions.question: When action is taken for an alcohol-related offense, a driver is placed under alcohol-restricted status for:
choices: 2, 3, 5, or 10 years, or lifetime depending on the offenses
questions.explanation: Alcohol-restricted status lasts 2, 3, 5, or 10 years — or lifetime for automobile homicide or felony DUI.

questions.question: A lifetime alcohol-restricted status results when a driver is convicted of automobile homicide or a felony DUI.
questions.explanation: Automobile homicide or a felony DUI conviction results in lifetime alcohol-restricted status.

questions.question: While on alcohol-restricted status, driving with a small amount of alcohol carries no penalty.
questions.explanation: False — any measurable alcohol while on alcohol-restricted status results in a one-year license revocation.

questions.question: Aggressive driving is best described as:
choices: Committing moving traffic offenses so as to endanger other persons or property
questions.explanation: Aggressive driving means committing moving traffic offenses so as to endanger other persons or property.

questions.question: To avoid distractions, you should set your navigation system and finish grooming before you start driving.
questions.explanation: Avoid distractions by setting navigation, eating, and grooming before you leave, and securing loose items.

questions.question: If you fail the driving skills test, you are expected to practice before taking another test.
questions.explanation: After a failed skills test you must practice before retaking it, and may wait days or weeks.

questions.question: At a four-way stop, which statement is correct?
choices: You always have the right-of-way as long as you signal
If you and another driver arrive at the same time, yield to the driver on your right
You may go before a vehicle that arrived and stopped before you
questions.explanation: Only the second is correct — yield to the driver on your right when you arrive together. Signaling never grants right-of-way, and you must yield to vehicles that arrived first.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about large trucks?
choices: They can stop in a shorter distance than cars
Because the driver sits up high, trucks have no blind spots
A loaded truck needs much more distance to stop than a car
questions.explanation: Only the third is true — a loaded tractor-trailer needs far more stopping distance than a car, and trucks have large blind spots (no-zones).

questions.question: Which statement about alcohol and driving is correct?
choices: A cup of coffee will sober you up enough to drive safely
Impairment begins with the very first drink
You can always drive safely as long as you feel fine
questions.explanation: Only the second is correct — impairment starts with the first drink. Coffee does not reverse impairment, and 'feeling fine' is not a safe measure.

questions.question: When you approach a steady red traffic light, you should:
choices: Come to a complete stop behind the stop line or crosswalk
Stop only if other cars are present
Slow down and roll through if the way looks clear
questions.explanation: Only the first is correct — always come to a complete stop behind the stop line or crosswalk at a steady red light.
